clustered_payloads.representative_payload: 锛咃紥锛曪紦小锝嶏絽锝旓絽锝掞紖锛掞紩锛掞紣锝忥綆锝咃綆锝勶紖锛掞紩锛揇锝冿綇锝庯絾锝夛綊锝嶏紖锛掞紩锛掞紭锛戯紖锛掞紩锛掞紮锛咃紥锛曪紦袝

final_rules.instructions: Detects double URL-encoded forms of '<', '>', '(', and ')'. This single rule consolidates multiple initial rules for efficiency. It targets multi-layer encoding bypasses before the final decoding pass.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated rules 10001, 10002, 10003, 10004 into a single, more efficient regex pattern using a non-capturing group. The score reflects the severity of finding any of these characters.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5](?:3[cC_eE]|2[89])" "msg:Double URL-encoded critical XSS characters (<, >, (, ))" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:11001;

final_rules.instructions: Detects double URL-encoded forms of characters frequently used in XSS payload construction, such as space, equals, colon, asterisk, and forward slash. Low score, designed for accumulation.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated rules 10005, 10006, 10023, 10024, 10038 for efficiency. The pattern covers multiple characters used in attribute assignment, comment injection, and protocol schemes.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5](?:2[0aAfF]|3[dD_aA])" "msg:Double URL-encoded XSS context characters (space, *, /, =, :)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:11002;

final_rules.instructions: Detects HTML decimal (e.g., <) and hexadecimal (e.g., <) encoded forms of the '<' character, including variants with leading zeros. Optional semicolon for parser tolerance.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Merged rules 10007 and 10034. Optimized regex to match both decimal and hex forms in a single pass.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#(?:x[0-9a-f]*3[cC]|[0-9]*60);?" "msg:HTML entity encoded less-than sign (dec/hex)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:11003;

final_rules.instructions: Detects HTML decimal (e.g., >) and hexadecimal (e.g., >) encoded forms of the '>' character. Complements rule 11003.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Merged rules 10008 and 10035. Optimized regex to match both decimal and hex forms in a single pass.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#(?:x[0-9a-f]*3[eE]|[0-9]*62);?" "msg:HTML entity encoded greater-than sign (dec/hex)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:11004;

final_rules.instructions: Detects HTML decimal (e.g., /) and hexadecimal (e.g., /) encoded forms of the '/' character, often used in closing tag obfuscation.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Merged rules 10009 and 10036. Optimized regex to match both decimal and hex forms in a single pass.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#(?:x[0-9a-f]*2[fF]|[0-9]*47);?" "msg:HTML entity encoded forward slash (dec/hex)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:11005;

final_rules.instructions: Detects UTF-8 byte sequences for Unicode characters used to bypass filters, including full-width ASCII equivalents (U+FF01-FF5E), Roman numerals (e.g., U+216C), and Cyrillic lookalikes (e.g., U+0421). This is a primary defense against the supplied bypassed payloads.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated and expanded rules 10025, 10026, 10027, 10028, 10029. Created a single high-performance regex targeting the UTF-8 byte patterns of multiple Unicode blocks used for evasion. Coverage expanded to include full-width numbers and symbols found in payloads.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?:\xef\xbc[\x81-\x9e])|(?:\xe2\x85[\xbc-\xbf]|\xe2\x86[\x80-\x8f])|(?:\xd0[\x90-\xaf\xa1\x86]|\xd1\x95)" "msg:Unicode homoglyph/full-width character abuse (XSS Evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:5" id:11006;

final_rules.instructions: Detects common HTML tags used in XSS after decoding and normalization. Word boundary (\b) prevents matching substrings. Allows for whitespace/slash injection between '<' and tag name.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated rules 10015, 10017, 10018, 10032 and added 'svg'. Created a more generic and efficient rule that targets multiple dangerous tags. Score is moderate; meant to be combined with event handler rules.

final_rules.instructions: Detects dangerous event handlers (`onload`, `onerror`, `onclick`) and the `srcdoc` attribute, which are strong indicators of XSS. High score intended for immediate blocking.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated rules 10011, 10012, 10013 into a more efficient pattern. Removed redundant logic for encoded equals signs, as that should be handled by normalization or separate encoding rules.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?:on(?:load|error|click)|srcdoc)[\s\x00]*=" "msg:High-risk XSS event handlers and attributes" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:11008;

final_rules.instructions: Detects `javascript:` and `vbscript:` URI schemes after normalization. Handles injected nulls or whitespace between the scheme and colon.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Merged rules 10019 and 10033. The combined rule is more efficient.

final_rules.instructions: Detects comment-based injection syntax (e.g., /*!50000*/), a technique often borrowed from SQLi to bypass keyword filters in other contexts like XSS.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Kept rule 10022. Optimized regex from `[0-9]+` to `\d+` for performance.

final_rules.instructions: Detects triple or deeper URL encoding by looking for three or more consecutive `%25` sequences. This is a strong anomaly signal for evasion attempts.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Kept rule 10031. Changed regex from `(?:%[2][5]){3,}` to the more readable and equivalent `(?:%25){3,}`.
final_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?:%25){3,}" "msg:Excessive URL encoding depth (>= 3 layers)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:6" id:10031;

final_rules.instructions: Detects the `alert(` pattern, a common proof-of-concept in XSS payloads. Score is moderate to avoid false positives on legitimate discussions of the function, but contributes to blocking when combined with other indicators.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Kept original rule 10020. It serves as a valuable secondary indicator.

generated_rules.instructions: Detects the double URL-encoded form of '<' (%253C). Naxsi decodes one layer automatically, so this rule catches the residual %25+3C pattern before full decode, targeting payloads like %253C that survive single-pass decoding. Score is additive; combine with other rules for threshold blocking.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][3][cC]" "msg:Double URL-encoded less-than sign (multi-layer encoding bypass)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10001;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects the double URL-encoded form of '>' (%253E). Mirrors rule 10001 for the closing angle bracket. Both rules together score 8 per payload pair, enough to trigger blocking when combined with CheckRule $XSS >= 8 BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][3][eE]" "msg:Double URL-encoded greater-than sign (multi-layer encoding bypass)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10002;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects double-encoded '(' (%2528). Common in alert(1) and similar XSS payloads across all clusters. Low individual score; accumulates with other XSS indicators.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][2][8]" "msg:Double URL-encoded open parenthesis (multi-layer encoding bypass)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10003;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects double-encoded ')' (%2529). Companion to rule 10003. Together these two rules add 4 to the XSS score per function-call pattern.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][2][9]" "msg:Double URL-encoded close parenthesis (multi-layer encoding bypass)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10004;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects double-encoded space (%2520) used to separate HTML tag attributes such as in payloads. Contributes to score accumulation.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][2][0]" "msg:Double URL-encoded space character (multi-layer encoding evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10005;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects double-encoded '=' (%253D) used in event handler attribute assignments such as onerror=, onload=, src=. Additive score.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][3][dD]" "msg:Double URL-encoded equals sign (multi-layer encoding bypass)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10006;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches HTML decimal entity < and < etc. for '<'. Targets Cluster 1 payload <Svg / OnLOAd>. The rx allows optional leading zeros in the decimal code.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#[0]*60" "msg:HTML entity encoded less-than sign" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10007;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches HTML decimal entity > for '>'. Pairs with rule 10007 to score tag-pair patterns from Cluster 1.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#[0]*62" "msg:HTML entity encoded greater-than sign" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10008;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches / (forward slash entity) used in closing tags like </script> and <svg/ ...> in Cluster 3 and Cluster 4 payloads. Low score, accumulates.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#[0]*47" "msg:HTML entity encoded forward slash" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10009;

generated_rules.instructions: Targets SVG tag used in Cluster 0 and Cluster 4 XSS payloads. The broad alternation captures ASCII 'svg' as well as multibyte Unicode lookalike sequences. Naxsi applies case-insensitive matching via rx. Combine with event handler rules.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:s[\xc0-\xff][\x80-\xbf]?[\x80-\xbf]?v[\xc0-\xff][\x80-\xbf]?[\x80-\xbf]?g|svg" "msg:SVG tag detection (including Unicode lookalike variants)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10010;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects onload= attribute after whitespace normalization. Targets Cluster 4 SVG onload payloads. Score 8 is sufficient alone to trigger blocking at the recommended threshold.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:onload[\x00-\x20]*=" "msg:onload event handler assignment" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10011;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects onerror= attribute used in patterns from Cluster 1. High score for immediate blocking.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:onerror[\x00-\x20]*=" "msg:onerror event handler assignment" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10012;

generated_rules.instructions: Covers both onclick= and onclick%3d / onclick%253d variants found in Cluster 1. The percent-hex alternative catches single and double encoded equals signs after Naxsi's automatic URL decode pass.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:onclick[\x00-\x20]*=|onclick%[0-9a-fA-F][0-9a-fA-F]" "msg:onclick event handler (encoded and plain)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10013;

generated_rules.instructions: Targets the ONtеХtЕNdЕхT event handler seen in Cluster 0 iframe payload. The rule uses the ASCII-normalized stem; Naxsi's regex engine will match after encoding normalization.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:ontextend|ontext" "msg:ontextend and similar uncommon event handlers" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:6" id:10014;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches decoded <script> with optional whitespace or slash between bracket and tag name. Targets Cluster 3 and Cluster 5 payloads. Naxsi decodes URL encoding before matching so %253cscript%253e becomes <script> after both decode passes.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:<[\x00-\x20/]*script[\x00-\x20>]" "msg:Script tag open (decoded form)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10015;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches decoded </script> closing tag variants from Cluster 3 and Cluster 5. Captures variations with whitespace injection between slash and tag name.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:<[\x00-\x20/]*\/[\x00-\x20]*script[\x00-\x20>]" "msg:Script closing tag (decoded form)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10016;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches decoded <iframe> from Cluster 0 and Cluster 2. Covers whitespace-injected variants and slash-separated forms like <iframe/src=...>.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:<[\x00-\x20/]*iframe[\x00-\x20>]" "msg:Iframe tag open (decoded form)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10017;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches decoded from Cluster 1 payloads. IMG tags are legitimate in rich-text contexts, so score is 6 rather than 8; combine with onerror/src rules for full block score.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:<[\x00-\x20/]*img[\x00-\x20>]" "msg:IMG tag open (decoded form)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:6" id:10018;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects decoded javascript: protocol used in iframe src= attributes from Cluster 0 and Cluster 2. Whitespace between scheme and colon is allowed by some parsers; the character class accounts for this.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:javascript[\x00-\x20]*:" "msg:javascript: URI scheme (decoded form)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10019;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects decoded alert( present across all clusters as the PoC payload function. Score 4 is additive; by itself it might appear in error messages so it should not block alone.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:alert[\x00-\x20]*\(" "msg:alert() function call in payload" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10020;

generated_rules.instructions: Targets src=javascript: combinations from Cluster 0 and Cluster 2 iframe payloads. Combines attribute assignment with the dangerous URI scheme.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:src[\x00-\x20]*=[\x00-\x20]*javascript" "msg:src attribute set to javascript URI" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10021;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects the /!50000/ or /*50000*/ comment-injection pattern used in Cluster 0 iframe src bypass payloads like <iframe /*50000*/ src=...>. This is a classic WAF-evasion technique borrowed from SQL injection comment injection.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:/\*!?[0-9]+\*/" "msg:CSS/JS comment-injection style integer in path (evasion via /!N*/ construct)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10022;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects %252A (double-encoded *) used in the /*50000*/ comment construction from Cluster 0. Low individual score; contributes to cumulative XSS score.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][2][aA]" "msg:Double URL-encoded asterisk (comment injection evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10023;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects %2521 (double-encoded !) used alongside the /*! construct in Cluster 0 evasion payloads. Accumulates with asterisk and numeric rules.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][2][1]" "msg:Double URL-encoded exclamation mark (comment injection evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10024;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects fullwidth Unicode equivalents of '<' (U+FF1C = EF BC 9C), '>' (U+FF1E = EF BC 9E), '{' (U+FF5B), '(' (U+FF08), ')' (U+FF09) in UTF-8 encoding. These are used in Cluster 1 Unicode obfuscation payloads.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:[\xef][\xbc][\x9c]|[\xef][\xbc][\x9e]|[\xef][\xbd][\x9b]|[\xef][\xbc][\x88]|[\xef][\xbc][\x89]" "msg:Fullwidth Unicode angle brackets and parentheses (Unicode lookalike evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10025;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches the fullwidth Latin character block (U+FF41-U+FF5A, U+FF21-U+FF3A) encoded as UTF-8 EF BD 81-BA. Used extensively across all clusters to substitute ASCII letters in tag names and event handlers.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:[\xef][\xbd][\x81-\xba]" "msg:Fullwidth Latin Unicode characters in payload (lookalike obfuscation)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:3" id:10026;

generated_rules.instructions: Targets Unicode Roman numerals (e.g. Ⅼ U+216C for L, Ⅰ for I) in the range U+217C-U+217F and related blocks, encoded as UTF-8. Used in payloads like onⅬОАD and аⅬеrt across clusters.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:[\xe2][\x85][\xbc-\xbf]|[\xe2][\x86][\x80-\x8f]" "msg:Roman numeral Unicode lookalikes used for letter substitution" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:3" id:10027;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects Cyrillic characters commonly used as Latin lookalikes: ѕ (U+0455, D1 95) for 's', С (U+0421, D0 A1) for 'C', І (U+0406, D0 86) for 'I', and Cyrillic uppercase block. Present throughout all cluster payloads.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:[\xd1][\x95]|[\xd0][\xa1]|[\xd0][\x86]|[\xd0][\x90-\xaf]" "msg:Cyrillic lookalike characters substituting Latin letters in HTML context" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:3" id:10028;

generated_rules.instructions: Targets specific Unicode format characters in the U+FFC1-U+FFC3 range used as letter substitutes in obfuscated payloads from Cluster 1. These rarely appear in legitimate traffic.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:[\xef][\xbf][\x81]|[\xef][\xbf][\x82]|[\xef][\xbf][\x83]" "msg:Unicode tag/format characters used for parser confusion" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:3" id:10029;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects the double-encoded open+close bracket combination %253C...%253E in a single parameter value, strongly indicative of HTML injection attempts. High score for near-certain block. More specific than individual bracket rules.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][3][cC].*%[2][5][3][eE]" "msg:Double-encoded HTML tag pair (full open+close bracket evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY" "s:$XSS:8" id:10030;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects triple or deeper URL encoding chains (e.g. %2525253C). Legitimate applications rarely produce encoding deeper than two layers; this is a strong anomaly signal for evasion attempts.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:(?:%[2][5]){3,}" "msg:Triple or higher URL encoding depth (excessive encoding anomaly)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:6" id:10031;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects decoded <body onload=...> construct referenced in Cluster 1 and OWASP evasion patterns. The \x00-\x20 class absorbs whitespace injection between attribute and value.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:body[\x00-\x20]+onload" "msg:BODY tag with onload event (decoded)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10032;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects vbscript: URI scheme referenced in the knowledge base evasion techniques. Provides defense-in-depth beyond the JavaScript-focused cluster payloads.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:vbscript[\x00-\x20]*:" "msg:VBScript URI scheme" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10033;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches < and < hex entity forms of '<'. Complements rule 10007 which covers decimal entities. Both must be present in a comprehensive XSS entity-encoding defense.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#[xX][0]*3[cC]" "msg:HTML hex entity encoded less-than sign" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10034;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches > hex entity form of '>'. Pairs with rule 10034 for hex entity tag detection.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#[xX][0]*3[eE]" "msg:HTML hex entity encoded greater-than sign" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:4" id:10035;

generated_rules.instructions: Matches / hex entity for '/' used in closing tag obfuscation. Complements decimal entity rule 10009.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:&#[xX][0]*2[fF]" "msg:HTML hex entity encoded forward slash" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:2" id:10036;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects the Set.constructor`alert(...)` ECMAScript 6 technique referenced in the OWASP knowledge base. The template literal form bypasses parenthesis-based filters.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:set\.constructor|constructor[\x00-\x20]*`" "msg:ECMAScript 6 constructor template literal XSS" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:8" id:10037;

generated_rules.instructions: Detects %253A (double-encoded ':') used in double-encoded javascript: URI schemes. Contributes to score when combined with javascript keyword rules.
generated_rules.rule: MainRule "rx:%[2][5][3][aA]" "msg:Double URL-encoded colon (protocol scheme evasion)" "mz:ARGS|URL|BODY|HEADERS" "s:$XSS:3" id:10038;

generated_rules.instructions: Whitelist rule exempting the IMG tag detection rule (10018) for parameters named 'content', which commonly accept rich HTML in CMS environments. Adjust the parameter name to match your application's rich-text field names. Do NOT whitelist in URL or HEADERS zones.
generated_rules.rule: BasicRule wl:10018 "mz:ARGS_NAMES:content|BODY_VAR:content";

generated_rules.instructions: Whitelist exempting the alert() detection rule (10020) for parameters named 'message' to reduce false positives in error message or notification fields. Review and restrict to specific known-safe parameters only.
generated_rules.rule: BasicRule wl:10020 "mz:ARGS_NAMES:message|BODY_VAR:message";

generated_rules.instructions: Primary blocking threshold: any combination of XSS-scored rules that accumulates 8 or more points triggers a BLOCK action. This threshold is calibrated so that a single high-severity rule (score 8) or two medium-severity rules (e.g. 4+4) cause blocking. Place this directive inside every protected location block.
generated_rules.rule: CheckRule "$XSS >= 8" BLOCK;

generated_rules.instructions: Secondary logging threshold: scores between 4 and 7 are logged for analyst review without blocking. Enables detection tuning and catches partial matches that may indicate probing. Reduce to BLOCK after a tuning period if false positive rate is acceptable.
generated_rules.rule: CheckRule "$XSS >= 4" LOG;
